text: Submits to John's baptism — suffer it to be so now, for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness
quote: Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness.

text: Rebuffs Satan with Scripture — man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God
quote: It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.

text: Refuses to test God — it is written again, thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God
quote: It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

text: Commands Satan to depart — thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve
quote: Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

text: Cleanses the leper with a touch — I will, be thou clean — then charges him to show himself to the priest as a testimony
quote: I will; be thou clean. See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

text: Responds to the centurion's request for his servant — I will come and heal him
quote: I will come and heal him.

text: Marvels at the centurion's faith — I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel
quote: Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.

text: Heals the centurion's servant by his word — go thy way, and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee
quote: Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee.

text: Authorizes demons to enter the herd of swine with a single word — Go
quote: Go.

text: Calls Matthew the tax collector from his booth — Follow me
quote: Follow me.

text: Quiets the mourners at Jairus's house — give place, the maid is not dead, but sleepeth
quote: Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth.

text: Tests the blind men's faith — believe ye that I am able to do this?
quote: Believe ye that I am able to do this?

text: Charges the healed blind men to silence — see that no man know it
quote: See that no man know it.

text: Identifies John the Baptist as the prophesied Elijah — if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come
quote: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.

text: Likens this generation to children in the markets — they reject both John's fasting and the Son of Man's eating, but wisdom is justified of her children
quote: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows, And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented. For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil. The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.

text: Pronounces woe on Chorazin and Bethsaida — Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes had they seen such mighty works
quote: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

text: Thanks the Father for hiding these things from the wise and revealing them unto babes — for so it seemed good in thy sight
quote: I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes. Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.

text: Defends his disciples on the Sabbath — David ate the shewbread, priests profane the sabbath blameless, and one greater than the temple is here
quote: Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him; How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests? Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless? But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.

text: Heals the man with the withered hand on the Sabbath — stretch forth thine hand
quote: Stretch forth thine hand.

text: The tree is known by its fruit — out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh, and a good man brings forth good treasure
quote: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit. O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh. A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.

text: Rebukes the sign-seekers — an evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and none shall be given but the sign of the prophet Jonas
quote: An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:

text: Nineveh and the Queen of the South will rise in judgment against this generation — for a greater than Jonas and Solomon is here
quote: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here. The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.

text: Redefines family — who is my mother and brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will
quote: Who is my mother? and who are my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren!

text: Quotes Isaiah's prophecy fulfilled in dull-hearted hearers — they hear but understand not, see but perceive not, lest they be converted
quote: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

text: Every scribe instructed in the kingdom is like a householder bringing forth from his treasure things new and old
quote: Have ye understood all these things? Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.

text: Notes the rejection at Nazareth — a prophet is not without honour, save in his own country and in his own house
quote: A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.

text: Tells the disciples to feed the multitude — they need not depart, give ye them to eat
quote: They need not depart; give ye them to eat.

text: Calls for the loaves and fishes before feeding the five thousand — bring them hither to me
quote: Bring them hither to me.

text: Reassures the disciples on the storm-tossed sea — be of good cheer, it is I, be not afraid
quote: Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.

text: Bids Peter walk on the water — Come
quote: Come.

text: Summons the multitude to attend his teaching on defilement — hear, and understand
quote: Hear, and understand:

text: Every plant which my heavenly Father hath not planted shall be rooted up
quote: Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.

text: Rebukes the disciples' dullness — are ye also yet without understanding that what enters the mouth goes into the belly?
quote: Are ye also yet without understanding? Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?

text: Concludes the teaching on defilement — these are the things that defile a man, but eating with unwashen hands does not
quote: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.

text: Defines his earthly mission — I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel
quote: I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

text: Tests the Canaanite woman — it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs
quote: It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it to dogs.

text: Praises the Canaanite woman's faith — O woman, great is thy faith, be it unto thee even as thou wilt
quote: O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt.

text: Expresses compassion on the multitude — they have continued with me three days and have nothing to eat, I will not send them away fasting
quote: I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.

text: Inquires before the feeding of the four thousand — how many loaves have ye?
quote: How many loaves have ye?

text: Begins his rebuke about discerning signs — when it is evening, ye say, it will be fair weather, for the sky is red
quote: When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.

text: Repeats the rebuke to sign-seekers — a wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and none shall be given but the sign of Jonas
quote: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas.

text: Rebukes the disciples for missing the point — O ye of little faith, do ye not remember the loaves of the five thousand and the four thousand?
quote: O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread? Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up? Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

text: Asks the disciples at Caesarea Philippi — whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
quote: Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?

text: Presses the disciples for their own confession — but whom say ye that I am?
quote: But whom say ye that I am?

text: Blesses Peter for his confession — flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven
quote: Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.

text: Rebukes Peter for opposing the cross — get thee behind me, Satan, thou savourest not the things of God but the things of men
quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.

text: Reassures the disciples after the Transfiguration — arise, and be not afraid
quote: Arise, and be not afraid.

text: Charges them to keep the vision secret — tell no man until the Son of man be risen again from the dead
quote: Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.

text: Affirms Elijah has already come — they knew him not but did unto him whatsoever they listed; likewise shall the Son of man suffer
quote: Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things. But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.

text: Laments the unbelief around him — O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I suffer you? Bring the boy hither to me
quote: O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.

text: Teaches Peter about the temple tax — kings take tribute from strangers, not their own children, yet to avoid offense, take a coin from a fish's mouth
quote: What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers? Then are the children free. Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.

text: Declares his mission to seek the lost — for the Son of man is come to save that which was lost
quote: For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.

text: Promises the twelve will sit on twelve thrones in the regeneration — when the Son of man sits in the throne of his glory, judging the twelve tribes of Israel
quote: Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

text: Reverses worldly ranks in the kingdom — but many that are first shall be last, and the last shall be first
quote: But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first.

text: Predicts his passion for the third time — the Son of man shall be betrayed, condemned, mocked, scourged, crucified, and the third day rise again
quote: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death, And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.

text: Stops for the two blind men outside Jericho — what will ye that I shall do unto you?
quote: What will ye that I shall do unto you?

text: Sends two disciples for the colt before the triumphal entry — say the Lord hath need of them, and straightway he will send them
quote: Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me. And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.

text: Cleanses the temple — my house shall be called the house of prayer, but ye have made it a den of thieves
quote: It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.

text: Defends the children's praise — have ye never read, out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
quote: Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?

text: Curses the fruitless fig tree — let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever
quote: Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever.

text: Counters the chief priests' challenge with a question — the baptism of John, whence was it, from heaven or of men?
quote: I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things. The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men?

text: Refuses to answer when they would not commit — neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
quote: Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

text: Silences the Pharisees with a question about Christ — whose son is he? If David in spirit calls him Lord, how is he his son?
quote: What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool? If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?

text: Foretells the destruction of the temple — there shall not be left here one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
quote: See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

text: Foretells his crucifixion at Passover — after two days is the feast, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified
quote: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

text: Defends the woman who anointed him — she has done a good work, pouring ointment for my burial, and shall be remembered wherever the gospel is preached
quote: Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me. For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always. For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial. Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.

text: Sends disciples to prepare the Passover — say the Master saith, my time is at hand, I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples
quote: Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.

text: Announces the betrayer at the Last Supper — verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me
quote: Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

text: Identifies Judas as the betrayer — he that dippeth his hand with me in the dish; woe to that man, it had been good for him not to have been born
quote: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me. The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born. Thou hast said.

text: Foretells the disciples' scattering — I will smite the shepherd and the sheep shall be scattered, but after I am risen I will go before you into Galilee
quote: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad. But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

text: Foretells Peter's three denials — this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice
quote: Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

text: Greets Judas at the betrayal — friend, wherefore art thou come?
quote: Friend, wherefore art thou come?

text: Rebukes the use of the sword — all that take the sword shall perish; I could call legions of angels, but the scriptures must be fulfilled
quote: Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword. Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels? But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be? Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me. But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.

text: Answers Pilate concerning kingship — Thou sayest
quote: Thou sayest.

text: Hold thy peace, and come out of him — Jesus rebukes the unclean spirit and casts it out with authority
quote: Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

text: Let us go into the next towns to preach there also — for this purpose I came forth
quote: Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.

text: I will; be thou clean — Jesus heals the leper with a word of compassion
quote: I will; be thou clean.

text: Tell no one, but show thyself to the priest and offer what Moses commanded for a testimony unto them
quote: See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

text: Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the tax collector to leave his post and become a disciple
quote: Follow me.

text: Have ye never read what David did when hungry — entering God's house and eating the shewbread reserved for priests
quote: Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him? How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?

text: Stand forth — is it lawful to do good or evil on the sabbath, to save life or to kill — stretch forth thine hand
quote: Stand forth. Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? Stretch forth thine hand.

text: Who is my mother, or my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will are my true family
quote: Who is my mother, or my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren!

text: Know ye not this parable? How then will ye know all parables — understanding the sower unlocks all kingdom teaching
quote: Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?

text: Let us pass over unto the other side — Jesus directs the disciples to cross the sea of Galilee
quote: Let us pass over unto the other side.

text: Peace, be still — Jesus rebukes the wind and commands the sea, and there was a great calm
quote: Peace, be still.

text: Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit — what is thy name? Jesus confronts the Gerasene demoniac's legion
quote: Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit. What is thy name?

text: Go home to thy friends and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee
quote: Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.

text: Who touched my clothes — Jesus perceives that virtue had gone out of him to heal the woman in the press
quote: Who touched my clothes?

text: Why make ye this ado, and weep? The damsel is not dead, but sleepeth — Jesus rebukes the mourners' unbelief
quote: Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.

text: Talitha cumi — Damsel, I say unto thee, arise — Jesus raises Jairus's daughter by the hand
quote: Talitha cumi;

text: A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, among his own kin, and in his own house
quote: A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.

text: Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while — Jesus calls the apostles to retreat with him
quote: Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while:

text: Give ye them to eat — how many loaves have ye? Go and see — Jesus prepares to feed the five thousand
quote: Give ye them to eat. How many loaves have ye? go and see.

text: Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid — Jesus reassures the disciples as he walks on the sea
quote: Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.

text: Laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men — washing pots and cups and many such things
quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.

text: Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand — Jesus calls the multitude to receive his teaching on defilement
quote: Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:

text: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear — Jesus presses the multitude to receive the teaching
quote: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

text: Are ye so without understanding? What enters from without cannot defile a man — but that which cometh out defileth him
quote: Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him; Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats? That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.

text: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man — the heart is the source of moral corruption
quote: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.

text: Let the children first be filled — it is not meet to take the children's bread and cast it unto the dogs
quote: Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.

text: For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter — Jesus rewards the Syrophoenician's faith
quote: For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.

text: Ephphatha — Be opened — Jesus heals the deaf man with an impeded speech impediment
quote: Ephphatha,

text: I have compassion on the multitude — they have been with me three days fasting and would faint by the way home
quote: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.

text: How many loaves have ye — Jesus asks before feeding the four thousand from seven loaves
quote: How many loaves have ye?

text: Why doth this generation seek after a sign? Verily, no sign shall be given unto this generation
quote: Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.

text: Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod — guard your hearts from their teaching
quote: Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.

text: Why reason ye because ye have no bread — having eyes see ye not, ears hear ye not — how is it ye do not understand
quote: Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened? Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember? When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? How is it that ye do not understand?

text: Neither go into the town nor tell it to any in the town — whom do men say that I am
quote: Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town. Whom do men say that I am?

text: Get thee behind me, Satan — for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men
quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

text: Elias verily cometh first and restoreth all things — and as it is written, Elias is indeed come and they did unto him as they listed
quote: Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought. But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.

text: What question ye with them — Jesus asks the scribes who were disputing with his disciples
quote: What question ye with them?

text: O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you, how long shall I suffer you — bring him unto me
quote: O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.

text: How long is it ago since this came unto him — Jesus asks the father of the demoniac boy
quote: How long is it ago since this came unto him?

text: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth — faith opens what unbelief shuts
quote: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.

text: Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him
quote: Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.

text: This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting — some deliverance requires deeper communion with God
quote: This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.

text: What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way — Jesus exposes the disciples' dispute over greatness
quote: What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?

text: Forbid him not, for none who works a miracle in my name can lightly speak evil of me — he that is not against us is for us
quote: Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me. For he that is not against us is on our part. For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.

text: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem — the Son of man shall be delivered, condemned, mocked, scourged, killed, and rise the third day
quote: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.

text: What would ye that I should do for you — Jesus questions James and John before granting their request
quote: What would ye that I should do for you?

text: What wilt thou that I should do unto thee — Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole — blind Bartimaeus receives his sight
quote: What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole.

text: Go into the village and find a colt tied — loose him and bring him; if any ask, say the Lord hath need of him
quote: Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him. And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.

text: No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever — Jesus curses the unfruitful fig tree as a sign against barren religion
quote: No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever.

text: Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer — but ye have made it a den of thieves
quote: Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.

text: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses
quote: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.

text: I will ask you one question — answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things — was John's baptism from heaven or men
quote: I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things. The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.

text: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things — Jesus refuses the chief priests' challenge
quote: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

text: Thou art not far from the kingdom — how say the scribes Christ is David's son when David himself by the Spirit calls him Lord
quote: Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David? For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool. David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.

text: Seest thou these great buildings — there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down
quote: Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

text: Let her alone — she hath wrought a good work on me, anointing my body for burial; this shall be told for a memorial of her
quote: Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me. For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always. She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying. Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.

text: Go into the city and follow a man bearing a pitcher of water — say the Master asks where the guestchamber is, and prepare the passover
quote: Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him. And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples? And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.

text: Verily I say unto you, one of you which eateth with me shall betray me — Jesus foretells the betrayal at the table
quote: Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.

text: It is one of the twelve that dippeth with me in the dish — woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed; better never born
quote: It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish. The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.

text: All ye shall be offended this night — I will smite the shepherd and the sheep shall be scattered — but after I am risen I will go before you into Galilee
quote: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered. But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.

text: Verily I say unto thee, this very night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice
quote: Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.

text: Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves — I was daily teaching in the temple, but the scriptures must be fulfilled
quote: Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me? I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.

text: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice — Peter remembers the word and weeps bitterly
quote: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.

text: Thou sayest it — Jesus answers Pilate concerning being the King of the Jews
quote: Thou sayest it.

text: How is it that ye sought me — wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?
quote: How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?

text: It is written — man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God
quote: It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.

text: Get thee behind me, Satan — thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve
quote: Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

text: It is said — thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God
quote: It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

text: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me — anointed to preach the gospel to the poor, heal the brokenhearted, and proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord
quote: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised, To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.

text: This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears
quote: This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.

text: No prophet is accepted in his own country — Elijah was sent to a widow of Sidon, and only Naaman the Syrian was cleansed
quote: Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country. Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country. But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land; But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow. And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.

text: Hold thy peace, and come out of him — Jesus rebukes the unclean spirit
quote: Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

text: I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also — for therefore am I sent
quote: I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.

text: Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught
quote: Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.

text: Fear not — from henceforth thou shalt catch men
quote: Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.

text: I will — be thou clean; go shew thyself to the priest and offer for thy cleansing as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them
quote: I will: be thou clean. but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

text: Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the publican from the receipt of custom
quote: Follow me.

text: Have ye not read what David did when hungered — he ate the shewbread which was lawful only for the priests
quote: Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him; How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?

text: Rise up and stand forth — is it lawful on the sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or destroy it? Stretch forth thy hand
quote: Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it? Stretch forth thy hand.

text: Why call ye me, Lord, Lord — and do not the things which I say?
quote: ¶ And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?

text: I have not found so great faith — no, not in Israel — Jesus marvels at the centurion
quote: I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.

text: Weep not — young man, I say unto thee, arise — Jesus raises the widow's son at Nain
quote: Weep not. Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.

text: This generation is like children in the marketplace — John came fasting and they said he had a devil; the Son of man came eating and they called him a glutton; but wisdom is justified of all her children
quote: Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like? They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept. For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil. The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners! But wisdom is justified of all her children.

text: Two debtors forgiven by a creditor — the one forgiven most loves most; this woman has loved much, while Simon offered no water, kiss, or oil
quote: Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty. And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most? Thou hast rightly judged. Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head. Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet. My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.

text: My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God and do it — let us go over unto the other side of the lake
quote: My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it. Let us go over unto the other side of the lake.

text: What is thy name? — Jesus questions the demoniac at Gadara
quote: What is thy name?

text: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee
quote: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee.

text: Who touched me? — somebody hath touched me, for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me
quote: Who touched me? Who touched me? Somebody hath touched me: for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me.

text: Daughter, be of good comfort — thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace
quote: Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.

text: Weep not — she is not dead, but sleepeth
quote: Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.

text: Maid, arise — Jesus raises Jairus' daughter
quote: Maid, arise.

text: Give ye them to eat — make them sit down by fifties in a company
quote: Give ye them to eat. Make them sit down by fifties in a company.

text: Whom say the people that I am? — Jesus questions his disciples while praying alone
quote: Whom say the people that I am?

text: O faithless and perverse generation — how long shall I be with you and suffer you? Bring thy son hither
quote: O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and suffer you? Bring thy son hither.

text: Forbid him not — he that is not against us is for us
quote: Forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.

text: Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of — the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives, but to save them
quote: Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of. For the Son of man is not come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.

text: I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven — rejoice not that spirits are subject to you, but that your names are written in heaven; the Father has hid these things from the wise and revealed them to babes
quote: I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven. Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you. Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven. I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.

text: What is written in the law? — how readest thou? — Jesus questions the lawyer
quote: What is written in the law? how readest thou?

text: Woe unto you, Pharisees — for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues and greetings in the markets
quote: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.

text: Woe unto you also, ye lawyers — for ye lade men with grievous burdens and touch them not with one of your fingers
quote: Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.

text: The wisdom of God sends prophets and apostles whom this generation shall slay — woe to lawyers who took away the key of knowledge and hindered those entering
quote: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation; From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation. Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.

text: Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?
quote: Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?

text: Let your loins be girded and your lights burning — blessed are servants whom the lord finds watching, for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not
quote: Let your loins be girded about, and your lights burning; And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately. Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them. And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants. And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through. Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.

text: But I have a baptism to be baptized with — and how am I straitened till it be accomplished
quote: But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!

text: When ye see a cloud rise out of the west ye say a shower comes; when the south wind blows ye say there will be heat — yet ye discern not this time
quote: When ye see a cloud rise out of the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it is. And when ye see the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it cometh to pass.

text: Suppose ye that these Galileans were sinners above all the Galileans because they suffered such things?
quote: Suppose ye that these Galilæans were sinners above all the Galilæans, because they suffered such things?

text: Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell — think ye that they were sinners above all in Jerusalem?
quote: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?

text: Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity — Jesus heals on the sabbath
quote: Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.

text: Thou hypocrite — doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or ass from the stall and lead him to watering?
quote: Thou hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering?

text: When the master shuts the door ye will say we ate and drank in thy presence — but he shall say, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all workers of iniquity
quote: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets. But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.

text: Tell that fox I cast out devils today and tomorrow — O Jerusalem, how often would I have gathered thy children as a hen gathers her brood, and ye would not
quote: Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected. Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem. O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen doth gather her brood under her wings, and ye would not! Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until the time come when ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

text: Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?
quote: Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?

text: Ye justify yourselves before men, but God knoweth your hearts — that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God
quote: Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.

text: It is easier for heaven and earth to pass than one tittle of the law to fail
quote: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.

text: When a servant comes from the field the master does not thank him — so likewise, when ye have done all things commanded, say, We are unprofitable servants who have done our duty
quote: But which of you, having a servant plowing or feeding cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat? And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink? Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not. So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we have done that which was our duty to do.

text: Go shew yourselves unto the priests — and as the ten lepers went, they were cleansed
quote: Go shew yourselves unto the priests.

text: Were there not ten cleansed — but where are the nine? None returned to give glory to God save this stranger
quote: Were there not ten cleansed? but where are the nine? There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.

text: The days will come when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man and shall not — go not after them when they say, See here or see there
quote: The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it. And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.

text: The Son of man shall be delivered to the Gentiles, mocked, scourged, and put to death — and the third day he shall rise again
quote: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.

text: What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? — receive thy sight; thy faith hath saved thee
quote: What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.

text: Zacchaeus, make haste and come down — for to day I must abide at thy house
quote: Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.

text: This day is salvation come to this house — forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham
quote: This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.

text: Go into the village and find a colt tied whereon never man sat — loose him and bring him; if any ask, say the Lord hath need of him
quote: Go ye into the village over against you; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring him hither. And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.

text: I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out
quote: I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.

text: If thou hadst known the things that belong unto thy peace — but now they are hid from thine eyes; thine enemies shall lay thee even with the ground because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation
quote: If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes. For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side, And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.

text: It is written, my house is the house of prayer — but ye have made it a den of thieves
quote: It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.

text: I will also ask you one thing — the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
quote: I will also ask you one thing; and answer me: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?

text: Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
quote: Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

text: How say they that Christ is David's son — when David himself in the Psalms calls him Lord, saying, Sit thou on my right hand till I make thine enemies thy footstool?
quote: How say they that Christ is David’s son? And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, Till I make thine enemies thy footstool. David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?

text: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come — there shall not be left one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
quote: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

text: Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat
quote: Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.

text: When ye are entered the city, a man bearing a pitcher of water shall meet you — follow him, and the goodman of the house will show you a large upper room furnished; there make ready
quote: Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in. And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples? And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.

text: Behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table — the Son of man goeth as determined, but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed
quote: ¶ But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table. And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!

text: Peter, the cock shall not crow this day before thou shalt thrice deny me — now he that hath a purse let him take it, for the scripture concerning me must be accomplished; it is enough
quote: I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me. When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one. For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end. It is enough.

text: Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
quote: Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?

text: Suffer ye thus far — be ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves? This is your hour, and the power of darkness
quote: Suffer ye thus far. Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves? When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.

text: Thou sayest it — Jesus answers Pilate concerning being King of the Jews
quote: Thou sayest it.

text: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves and your children — if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
quote: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children. For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck. Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us. For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?

text: What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
quote: What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?

text: What things? — Jesus draws out the Emmaus disciples' account of Jesus of Nazareth
quote: What things?

text: O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken — ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
quote: O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?

text: Peace be unto you — Jesus stands among the disciples after the resurrection
quote: Peace be unto you.

text: Have ye here any meat? — the risen Jesus asks for food to confirm his bodily presence
quote: Have ye here any meat?

text: He asks the first disciples what they seek and invites them to come and see where he dwells
quote: What seek ye? Come and see.

text: He renames Simon son of Jonah as Cephas, meaning a stone, marking him with a new identity
quote: Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas,

text: He greets Nathanael as a true Israelite without guile and reveals he saw him under the fig tree before Philip called
quote: Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile! Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.

text: He promises Nathanael will see greater things — heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man
quote: Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.

text: He tells his mother at Cana that his hour has not yet come
quote: Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.

text: He commands the servants to fill the waterpots with water and to draw out and bear it to the governor of the feast
quote: Fill the waterpots with water. Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast.

text: He drives out the dove sellers from the temple, declaring that his Father's house must not be made a house of merchandise
quote: Take these things hence; make not my Father’s house an house of merchandise.

text: He rebukes Nicodemus the master of Israel for not knowing these things, testifying of what he has seen and warning that those who reject earthly truths cannot grasp heavenly ones
quote: Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness. If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?

text: He declares that condemnation comes because light entered the world but men loved darkness, while those who do truth come to the light so their deeds are shown to be wrought in God
quote: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil. For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved. But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.

text: He asks the Samaritan woman at the well to give him a drink
quote: Give me to drink.

text: He tells the woman that if she knew the gift of God and who was speaking with her, she would have asked him for living water
quote: If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.

text: He says whoever drinks of this well water will thirst again, contrasting it with the living water he gives
quote: Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:

text: He tells the woman to call her husband and reveals he knows she has had five husbands and the man she now has is not her husband
quote: Go, call thy husband, and come hither. Thou hast well said, I have no husband: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.

text: He tells the woman the hour comes when worship will be neither at this mountain nor Jerusalem, and that salvation is of the Jews
quote: Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father. Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.

text: He tells the disciples he has meat to eat that they know nothing of
quote: I have meat to eat that ye know not of.

text: His meat is to do the will of him who sent him and to finish his work — the fields are white for harvest, and reapers gather fruit unto life eternal so sower and reaper rejoice together
quote: My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work. Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest. And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together. And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth. I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.

text: He rebukes the nobleman, saying that unless they see signs and wonders, they will not believe
quote: Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.

text: He sends the nobleman home with the word that his son lives, healing the boy at a distance
quote: Go thy way; thy son liveth.

text: He asks the lame man at the pool of Bethesda whether he wants to be made whole
quote: Wilt thou be made whole?

text: He commands the lame man to rise, take up his bed, and walk
quote: Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.

text: He finds the healed man in the temple and warns him to sin no more lest a worse thing come upon him
quote: Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.

text: He defends his Sabbath healing by declaring that his Father works until now and he himself works
quote: My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.

text: The Son can do nothing of himself but only what he sees the Father do, for the Father loves the Son and shows him all things, and will show greater works
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise. For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.

text: He judges only as the Father directs and points to multiple witnesses — John the Baptist, his works given by the Father, and the Father himself — that they might be saved
quote: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me. If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true. ¶ There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true. Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth. But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved. He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light. ¶ But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me. And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape. And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.

text: He receives no honor from men but knows they lack the love of God; they reject him who comes in the Father's name yet would receive another, and Moses himself accuses them
quote: I receive not honour from men. But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you. I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive. How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only? Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.

text: He asks Philip where they shall buy bread to feed the great multitude that has come to him
quote: Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?

text: He instructs the disciples to make the men sit down before the feeding of the five thousand
quote: Make the men sit down.

text: He tells the disciples to gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing be lost
quote: Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.

text: He calls out to the terrified disciples on the sea, saying it is I — be not afraid
quote: It is I; be not afraid.

text: He rebukes the crowd for seeking him only because they ate the loaves and tells them to labor not for perishing meat but for the meat that endures unto everlasting life
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled. Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.

text: He declares that the work of God is to believe on the one whom God has sent
quote: This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.

text: Moses did not give the true bread from heaven; the Father gives it, and the bread of God comes down from heaven and gives life unto the world
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven. For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.

text: He tells the crowd that even though they have seen him, they do not believe
quote: But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.

text: He came down from heaven not to do his own will but the will of him who sent him, and the Father's will is that he lose none given to him but raise them up at the last day
quote: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me. And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.

text: He tells the crowd not to murmur among themselves
quote: Murmur not among yourselves.

text: Everyone taught of God and who learns of the Father comes to him, no man has seen the Father except the one from God, and whoever believes on him has everlasting life
quote: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me. Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father. Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.

text: Their fathers ate manna in the wilderness and died, but this is the bread that comes down from heaven so that one may eat of it and not die
quote: Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead. This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.

text: Unless one eats the flesh of the Son of Man and drinks his blood, he has no life in him
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.

text: As the living Father sent him and he lives by the Father, so the one who eats him shall live by him; this bread from heaven gives life forever, unlike the manna
quote: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me. This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.

text: He asks the murmuring disciples whether his teaching offends them, and what if they should see the Son of Man ascend up where he was before
quote: Doth this offend you? What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?

text: He declares that some among them do not believe, and that no man can come to him unless it is given by the Father
quote: But there are some of you that believe not. Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.

text: He asks the twelve whether they also will go away
quote: Will ye also go away?

text: He reminds the twelve that he has chosen them, yet one of them is a devil
quote: Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?

text: He tells his brothers his time is not yet come while theirs is always ready, that the world hates him because he testifies its works are evil, and they should go up to the feast without him
quote: My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready. The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil. Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.

text: His doctrine is not his own but his that sent him, and any man willing to do God's will shall know whether the doctrine is of God; he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true
quote: My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me. If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself. He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him. Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?

text: He defends his Sabbath healing by appealing to the lawful practice of circumcision on the Sabbath, asking why they are angry that he made a man whole
quote: I have done one work, and ye all marvel. Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man. If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?

text: He cries in the temple that he is not come of himself but is from him who sent him; he knows the Father, but they do not
quote: Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not. But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.

text: He declares that yet a little while he is with them and then goes to him who sent him, and where he goes they cannot come
quote: Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me. Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.

text: He cries on the great day of the feast that if any man thirsts, let him come to him and drink
quote: If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

text: He tells the accusers of the woman caught in adultery that he who is without sin among them should cast the first stone
quote: He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.

text: He asks the woman where her accusers are and tells her that neither does he condemn her — go and sin no more
quote: Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee? Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.

text: They judge after the flesh, but he judges no man; yet if he judges, his judgment is true because he is not alone but with the Father who sent him
quote: Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man. And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.

text: He tells them they neither know him nor his Father; if they had known him, they would have known the Father also
quote: Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.

text: He says he goes his way and they shall seek him and die in their sins, for where he goes they cannot come
quote: I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.

text: Unless they believe that I am he, they shall die in their sins; he is the same he said from the beginning, and he speaks to the world what he has heard from the one who sent him
quote: I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins. Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning. I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.

text: If they continue in his word, they are his disciples indeed
quote: If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;

text: Whoever commits sin is the servant of sin, and the servant does not abide in the house forever, but the Son abides forever
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin. And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.

text: Though they are Abraham's seed, they seek to kill him because his word has no place in them; they are of their father the devil — a murderer and liar from the beginning — and reject the truth he speaks from God
quote: I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you. I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father. If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham. But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham. Ye do the deeds of your father. If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me. Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word. Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it. And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not. Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me? He that is of God heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.

text: He has no devil but honors his Father; he seeks not his own glory, and if any man keeps his saying, he shall never see death
quote: I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me. And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth. Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.

text: His honor comes not from himself but from the Father; though they claim God as theirs, they do not know him, but he knows the Father and keeps his saying
quote: If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God: Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.

text: Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but the works of God should be made manifest in him; he must work the works of him that sent him while it is day, for night comes when no man can work
quote: Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him. I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.

text: He sends the man born blind to wash in the pool of Siloam, and the man returns seeing
quote: Go, wash in the pool of Siloam,

text: He finds the cast-out man and asks him whether he believes on the Son of God
quote: Dost thou believe on the Son of God?

text: He reveals himself to the healed man, declaring that the one who talks with him is the Son of God
quote: Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.

text: He has come for judgment into this world, that those who do not see might see, and those who see might be made blind
quote: For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.

text: He who does not enter the sheepfold by the door is a thief and robber, but the shepherd enters by the door, calls his own sheep by name, and they follow him because they know his voice — they will not follow a stranger
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber. But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep. To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out. And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice. And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.

text: All who came before him are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them
quote: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.

text: The thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy, but he has come that they might have life and have it more abundantly
quote: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.

text: The hireling, who is not the shepherd, sees the wolf coming and flees because he does not own the sheep, leaving them to be caught and scattered
quote: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep. The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.

text: As the Father knows him, so he knows the Father, and he lays down his life for the sheep; he has other sheep not of this fold whom he must bring, that there shall be one fold and one shepherd
quote: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep. And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.

text: They do not believe because they are not of his sheep, as he had said unto them
quote: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.

text: He asks for which of the many good works shown them from the Father do they seek to stone him
quote: Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?

text: He cites the law that called them gods to whom the word of God came, asking why they accuse him of blasphemy for saying he is the Son of God whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world
