quote: Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods? If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken; Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?

text: Lazarus' sickness is not unto death but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified by it.
quote: This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.

text: He calls the disciples to go back into Judaea again despite the danger awaiting him there.
quote: Let us go into Judæa again.

text: He who walks in the day does not stumble because he sees the light of this world; but he who walks in the night stumbles for he has no light — Lazarus sleeps, and Jesus goes to wake him.
quote: Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world. But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him. Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.

text: He tells them plainly that Lazarus is dead, and is glad he was not there so that they might believe — let us go to him.
quote: Lazarus is dead. And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.

text: He assures Martha that her brother shall rise again.
quote: Thy brother shall rise again.

text: He asks the mourners where they have laid Lazarus.
quote: Where have ye laid him?

text: He commands that the stone be removed, reminds Martha that belief sees God's glory, thanks the Father who always hears him, calls Lazarus forth from the tomb, and orders that he be loosed and let go.
quote: Take ye away the stone. Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God? Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me. And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me. Lazarus, come forth. Loose him, and let him go.

text: He defends Mary's anointing — she has kept this for the day of his burying; the poor are always with you, but he will not always be present.
quote: Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this. For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.

text: The hour has come that the Son of man should be glorified.
quote: The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.

text: He who loves his life loses it, while he who hates his life in this world keeps it eternal; whoever serves Jesus must follow him — though his soul is troubled, he will not ask the Father to save him from this hour, but prays that the Father's name be glorified.
quote: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal. If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour. Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour. Father, glorify thy name.

text: The voice from heaven came for the people's sake — now is the judgment of this world, and the prince of this world shall be cast out.
quote: This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes. Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.

text: Yet a little while is the light with you — walk while you have the light lest darkness overtake you, and believe in the light that you may become children of light.
quote: Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth. While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light.

text: He who believes in Jesus believes in the One who sent him, and seeing him is seeing the Father; he came as light into the world so that believers should not abide in darkness, and came not to judge but to save.
quote: He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me. And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me. I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness. And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

text: He has not spoken from himself, for the Father who sent him gave commandment what to say, and that commandment is life everlasting.
quote: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak. And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.

text: What he is doing Peter does not now understand but will know later — and unless Peter is washed, he has no part with Jesus.
quote: What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter. If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.

text: He who is washed needs only to wash his feet and is clean every whit — and the disciples are clean, but not all of them.
quote: He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.

text: He asks if they understand what he has done; they rightly call him Master and Lord, for so he is.
quote: Know ye what I have done to you? Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.

text: He has chosen the Twelve, yet scripture must be fulfilled that one who eats bread with him will lift his heel against him; he tells them beforehand so they may believe — and one of them shall betray him.
quote: ¶ I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me. Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he. Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me. Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

text: He identifies the betrayer as the one to whom he gives the dipped sop, then tells Judas, what you do, do quickly.
quote: He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. That thou doest, do quickly.

text: Now is the Son of man glorified and God glorified in him; little children, yet a little while he is with them, and where he goes they cannot come.
quote: Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him. If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him. Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.

text: Where he goes Peter cannot follow now, but shall follow afterwards.
quote: Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.

text: He questions Peter's claim to lay down his life and foretells that the cock shall not crow until Peter has denied him three times.
quote: Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.

text: Where he is going they know, and the way they know.
quote: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.

text: If they had known him, they would have known the Father also — and from now on they know him and have seen him.
quote: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.

text: He who believes in Jesus shall do the works he does, and greater works, because he goes to the Father.
quote: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.

text: If you love me, keep my commandments.
quote: ¶ If ye love me, keep my commandments.

text: He will not leave them comfortless but will come to them; because he lives they shall live also, and shall know that he is in the Father, they in him, and he in them — and the one who keeps his commandments loves him and shall be loved by the Father.
quote: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you. Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also. At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you. He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.

text: If a man loves him he will keep his words, and the Father will love him, and they will come and make their abode with him; the word they hear is not his own but the Father's.
quote: If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him. He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me. These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.

text: He has told them beforehand that they might believe; the prince of this world is coming and has nothing in him, but the world must know that he loves the Father — arise, let us go.
quote: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe. Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me. But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.

text: They are already clean through the word he has spoken to them.
quote: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.

text: If the world hates them they should know it hated him first; because they are not of the world but chosen out of it, the world hates them.
quote: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you. If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

text: The world will persecute them for his name's sake because it does not know the Father; if he had not come and done unprecedented works among them they would have no sin, but now they have seen and hated both him and the Father — fulfilling the scripture, they hated me without a cause.
quote: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me. If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin. He that hateth me hateth my Father also. If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father. But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.

text: And they also shall bear witness, because they have been with him from the beginning.
quote: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.

text: He has spoken these things so that they should not be offended.
quote: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.

text: Persecutors will act against them because they have known neither the Father nor him; he tells them now so they will remember when the time comes — now he goes to the One who sent him, and sorrow has filled their hearts.
quote: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me. But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you. But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou? But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

text: He has many things yet to say to them, but they cannot bear them now.
quote: I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.

text: All things the Father has are his, therefore the Spirit will take of his and show it to them — a little while and they shall not see him, and again a little while and they shall see him, because he goes to the Father.
quote: All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you. A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.

text: They shall weep and lament while the world rejoices, but their sorrow shall be turned to joy — like a woman in travail who forgets her anguish for joy that a child is born — and their joy no one will take from them.
quote: Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me? Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy. A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world. And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.

text: He has spoken in proverbs, but the time comes when he will show them plainly of the Father; in that day they shall ask in his name.
quote: These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father. At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:

text: He asks if they truly believe now, for the hour comes when they shall be scattered every man to his own and leave him alone — yet he is not alone, for the Father is with him.
quote: Do ye now believe? Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

text: The disciples have known that all things given to him are from the Father, for he gave them the Father's words, which they received and believed that the Father sent him.
quote: Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee. For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.

text: All things that are his are the Father's, and all that are the Father's are his — and he is glorified in his disciples.
quote: And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.

text: While he was with them he kept them in the Father's name, and none was lost but the son of perdition; now he comes to the Father so they might have his joy fulfilled in themselves, though the world has hated them because they are not of it, even as he is not.
quote: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled. And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves. I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

text: His disciples are not of the world, even as he is not of the world.
quote: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

text: O righteous Father, the world has not known you, but he has known you, and these have known that you sent him.
quote: O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.

text: Going forth to those who came to arrest him, he asks, Whom do you seek?
quote: Whom seek ye?

text: He commands Peter to put up his sword — shall he not drink the cup the Father has given him?
quote: Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?

text: Before the high priest he answers that he spoke openly to the world, taught in synagogue and temple, and said nothing in secret — let those who heard him be asked, for they know what he said.
quote: I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing. Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.

text: If he has spoken evil, let the officer bear witness of the evil; but if he has spoken well, why is he struck?
quote: If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?

text: He asks Pilate whether he says this of himself, or whether others have told him concerning Jesus.
quote: Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?

text: His kingdom is not of this world; if it were, his servants would fight to keep him from being delivered to the Jews — but his kingdom is not from here.
quote: My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.

text: Pilate would have no power against him at all unless it were given from above; therefore the one who delivered him to Pilate has the greater sin.
quote: Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.

text: Appearing again to the disciples eight days later with Thomas present, he greets them, Peace be unto you.
quote: Peace be unto you.

text: From the shore he asks the fishermen if they have any meat, and tells them to cast the net on the right side of the ship, where they will find a catch.
quote: Children, have ye any meat? Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find.

text: He instructs them to bring of the fish which they have just caught.
quote: Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.

text: He invites the disciples, Come and dine.
quote: Come and dine.

text: Peter recalls the word of the Lord: John baptized with water, but you shall be baptized with the Holy Spirit
quote: John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.

text: To the angel of the church in Ephesus: these things saith he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand and walketh in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks
quote: Unto the angel of the church of Ephesus write; These things saith he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand, who walketh in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks;

text: Yet this you have to your credit: you hate the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate
quote: But this thou hast, that thou hatest the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.

text: To the angel of the church in Smyrna: these things saith the first and the last, who was dead and is alive
quote: And unto the angel of the church in Smyrna write; These things saith the first and the last, which was dead, and is alive;

text: To the angel of the church in Pergamos: these things saith he which hath the sharp two-edged sword
quote: And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write; These things saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges;

text: To the angel of the church in Thyatira: these things saith the Son of God, whose eyes are like a flame of fire and whose feet are like fine brass
quote: And unto the angel of the church in Thyatira write; These things saith the Son of God, who hath his eyes like unto a flame of fire, and his feet are like fine brass;

text: To the rest in Thyatira who do not hold this teaching and have not known the depths of Satan: I will lay on you no other burden
quote: But unto you I say, and unto the rest in Thyatira, as many as have not this doctrine, and which have not known the depths of Satan, as they speak; I will put upon you none other burden.

text: A few in Sardis have not defiled their garments — they shall walk with me in white, for they are worthy
quote: Thou hast a few names even in Sardis which have not defiled their garments; and they shall walk with me in white: for they are worthy.

text: To the angel of the church in Philadelphia: these things saith he that is holy and true, who hath the key of David — he opens and no one shuts, he shuts and no one opens
quote: And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write; These things saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the key of David, he that openeth, and no man shutteth; and shutteth, and no man openeth;

text: To the angel of the church of the Laodiceans: these things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God
quote: And unto the angel of the church of the Laodiceans write; These things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;
